content_en:
On the second day as well the Mujahideen triumphed over the enemy though it was nota decisive victory. The third day too it seemed as if the war was not going to end. The Iranian Empire continued to send

in fresh troop everyday. The experienced general Qa‘q@’ bin ‘Amr adopted a new strategy on the third day. He told a large number of his warriors to move towards Syria and stop there at a suitable spot. With the first rays of dawn divisions consisting of a hundred horsemen each should race their horses fast and reach the battlefield one by one. When these brigades, shouting ‘Allaého-Akbar’ reached, one after another, raising a lot of dust, the Iranian army got terrified. They thought fresh reinforcements were coming in from Syria. But the Iranian Empire and his famous General, Rustam, were not prepared to admit defeat. They stuck stubbornly in the battlefield. The army had been positioned on the left and right of the elephants to protect them. But ‘Amr bin Ma’d Yakrab after informing his companions, dared to attack the elephants. He did get injured in the process, but succeeded in cutting off the trunk of an elephant. All the other elephants stampeded, fleeing backwards. And as a result, they trampled down their own soldiers. In spite of this it did not seem that the Muslim army could defeat the Iranians, who still had a very large number of soldiers.

Qa’‘qa’ bin ‘Amr 4 formed a special division of ace riders and leading them himself, he advanced towards the Iranian General, Rustam. He was seated on his throne directing his men to fight. With one intense attack, he succeeded in reaching the throne of the Iranian General. He jumped down from his throne and tried to fight against them for somé time, but when he saw it was futile, he fled. When he saw a stream in front of him, he jumped into it to save himself; one of the Mujahideen, Hilal, who was chasing him jumped in after him and got stranglehold on him. He pulled him out of the stream by his legs, and chopped off his head with his sword. It was then announced that the Iranian General had been killed. When the Iranian soldiers heard this they lost hope, and started to scamper from the field of battle. The Mujahideen chased them for a long distance and the battlefield was left empty. Thus, the Muslim army won.

Though Sa’d bin Abi Wagas . did not take an active part in the battle because of his illness, he was continuously issuing orders, and for all practical purposes, commanding the forces. He was sitting on one of the balconies of the fort and could view the whole battlefield, the positions of the various divisions and the movements of the two armies.

In this war, besides the faith and fervour of the Mujahideen what could be seen was the excellent planning of the Commander. Hence the credit for the victory in the War of Qadisiyah went to the great General, Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas >.

It has been the practice in wars that the General is always held responsible for the success or defeat of the army, whether he plays an active personal role in the battle or not. In spite of his serious illness, Sa‘d bin Abi Waqds sat on the balcony and kept giving orders which helped the Mujahideen immensely. He kept calling out:

“Mujahideen, advance towards the left! Now advance towards the right! Mughaira you move forward now. Jareer you retreat a little now.

No’man attack with your sword. Ash‘ath move forward and attack now! And O Qa’qa’, move forward fast now!

O lovers of Islam and loyal followers of the Prophet 3%, well done! See that you do not give up and stop! The world’s eyes are on you, envying you and watching your exploits of bravery, daring, fervour and desire for martyrdom!

O Mujahideen! Victory is just a couple of steps away and waiting for you.”

The Muslims got a resounding victory in the War of Qadisiyah, but their victorious march was far from over.

Next the Muslim army faced the Iranians in Madyan. In this battle too the Iranians came very well equipped and armed to the teeth. One major problem for the Muslim forces here was that the river Dajlah lay between them and the Iranian army. The Iranians had deployed their army along the banks of the river and adopted a defensive position. Further, in order to protect themselves, they had destroyed all the

bridges on the river. In order to have a decisive encounter it was necessary that the Muslim army make the first move and advance. General Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas 4 was in a dilemma. How should he cross the river? Even if they did succeed in crossing the river, it was very possible the enemy would launch an attack even before they reached the other bank of the river. In that case the enemy would be at an advantage, because the Muslim army would still be in the river. After a lot of thought and planning he decided to form two armies. He ordered one segment to cross the river under the command of ‘Aasim bin ‘Amr 4. He ordered the other segment of the army to cross the river some distance away; this second section he placed under the Command of Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr #.

The purpose of his strategy was to keep the enemy busy and involved with the first section of the army reaching the bank. The other section of the army would start crossing after some time had elapsed, and make a deadly attack on the enemy as soon as it reached the shores. This strategy was so successful that it stunned people and aroused their admiration with its ingenuity. Sa’d bin Abi Waqas 4 had created military history with this novel tactical move.

With full faith in Allah Almighty he had asked his men to step into the river. Their lips were busy with the incantation of the Name of Allah 3B. They were reciting the Ayah:

“Allah’s Help and Support is enough for me.”

The Mujaliideen had stepped into the deep waters of the river Dajlah fearlessly. The world saw that they were chatting with each other as if they were casually strolling on dry land. Salaman Al-Farsi was also a soldier in this army. He expressed beautiful inspiring thoughts on this occasion, which deserve to be written in letters of gold in the annals of history. He said:

“Islam is a religion which is perfect and descended from the heavens. | swear by the Almighty Allah, for the believers of Islam, the river is as negligible as the desert. I swear by the Being in Whose Power lies my life that as many Mujahideen will step out safely from the river as those who stepped into the river. The deep waters of the river can harm them in no way.”

History is witness to the fact that not even a rope was lost by the Mujahideen when they crossed the river. A cup fell from the hand of one Mujahid. He asked his companions to help him find it; and what should they see but that the cup came floating towards them ona wave. All Praise is for Allah! Alftah is Great!

Seeing this fearful awe-inspiring sight, the Iranians started screaming, that giants had come to swallow them up and fled in panic. Their General, however, remained resolute. A few scattered men stood by him, but the Iranians faced a total rout. They were decimated the very first day and the important city of Madyan with its royal palaces was occupied. The Iranian Empire had already fled to Yazdagar. All the goods and treasures taken from Madyan were sent to A]-Madinah.

When Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas + entered the city of Madyan at the head of his army, he found the city totally deserted in deadly silence. He was so deeply moved that he recited the following Verses:

“How many of gardens and springs that they left behind. And green crops (fields) and goodly places. And comforts of life wherein they used to take delight. Thus (it was)! And We made other people inherit them (i.e. We made the Children of Israel to inherit the kingdom of Egypt).” (44:25-28)

As soon as Madyan was captured the whole of Iraq came under the sway of the Muslims. The Islamic government declared a general amnesty, and people who had fled their houses out of fear were asked to return and settle in their homes. As soon as people heard this announcement they came back and their homes, their wealth and their honour were protected.

After Iraq came under the complete control of the government, Sa’d bin Abi Waqas 4% was appointed governor of the country. It is as difficult to organise a government and run the country as it is to conquer it. Sa’d bin Abi Waqas % had impressed people with his military skill and courage; now as Governor and administrator, with his excellent planning and organisational skills, he made Iraq an ideal and exemplary state in a short time.

The sense of justice and fair play, the magnanimity and good treatment of all people made the people happy and contented. But some of the Koofis who were stubborn trouble mongers complained to the government at the centre that their governor did not conduct prayers in a proper manner and displays laxity in devotions.
content_id:
Pada hari kedua pun para Mujahidin meraih kemenangan atas musuh meskipun bukan kemenangan yang menentukan. Hari ketiga pun tampaknya perang tidak akan berakhir. Kekaisaran Iran terus mengirimkan

pasukan segar setiap hari. Jenderal berpengalaman Qa'qa' bin 'Amr mengadopsi strategi baru pada hari ketiga. Ia memerintahkan sejumlah besar prajuritnya untuk bergerak menuju Suriah dan berhenti di tempat yang sesuai. Dengan sinar fajar pertama, divisi-divisi yang terdiri dari seratus penunggang kuda masing-masing harus memacu kuda mereka dengan cepat dan mencapai medan pertempuran satu per satu. Ketika brigade-brigade ini, meneriakkan 'Allahu Akbar', tiba satu demi satu sambil mengangkat banyak debu, tentara Iran menjadi ketakutan. Mereka mengira bala bantuan segar datang dari Suriah. Namun Kekaisaran Iran dan Jenderalnya yang terkenal, Rustam, tidak siap mengakui kekalahan. Mereka bertahan dengan keras kepala di medan pertempuran. Pasukan telah ditempatkan di kiri dan kanan gajah-gajah untuk melindungi mereka. Namun 'Amr bin Ma'd Yakrab setelah memberitahu rekan-rekannya, berani menyerang gajah-gajah tersebut. Ia memang terluka dalam proses itu, tetapi berhasil memotong belalai seekor gajah. Semua gajah lainnya panik, melarikan diri ke belakang. Dan akibatnya, mereka menginjak-injak prajurit mereka sendiri. Meskipun demikian, tampaknya tentara Muslim belum bisa mengalahkan orang-orang Iran yang masih memiliki jumlah prajurit yang sangat besar.

Qa'qa' bin 'Amr radhiyallahu 'anhu membentuk divisi khusus dari penunggang kuda terbaik dan memimpin mereka sendiri, ia maju menuju Jenderal Iran, Rustam. Rustam sedang duduk di singgasananya mengarahkan pasukannya untuk berperang. Dengan satu serangan dahsyat, ia berhasil mencapai singgasana Jenderal Iran. Rustam melompat turun dari singgasananya dan mencoba melawan mereka untuk beberapa waktu, tetapi ketika ia melihat itu sia-sia, ia melarikan diri. Ketika ia melihat sungai di depannya, ia melompat ke dalamnya untuk menyelamatkan diri; salah seorang Mujahidin, Hilal, yang mengejarnya melompat masuk setelahnya dan mencekiknya. Ia menariknya keluar dari sungai dengan memegang kakinya, dan memenggal kepalanya dengan pedang. Kemudian diumumkan bahwa Jenderal Iran telah terbunuh. Ketika prajurit Iran mendengar ini, mereka kehilangan harapan dan mulai berlarian meninggalkan medan pertempuran. Para Mujahidin mengejar mereka dalam jarak yang jauh dan medan pertempuran dibiarkan kosong. Dengan demikian, tentara Muslim menang.

Meskipun Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas radhiyallahu 'anhu tidak ikut serta secara aktif dalam pertempuran karena sakitnya, ia terus-menerus mengeluarkan perintah, dan untuk semua tujuan praktis, ia yang memimpin pasukan. Ia duduk di salah satu balkon benteng dan dapat melihat seluruh medan pertempuran, posisi berbagai divisi, dan pergerakan kedua pasukan.

Dalam perang ini, selain keimanan dan semangat para Mujahidin, yang dapat dilihat adalah perencanaan yang sangat baik dari sang Komandan. Oleh karena itu, penghargaan atas kemenangan dalam Perang Qadisiyah diberikan kepada jenderal besar, Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas radhiyallahu 'anhu.

Sudah menjadi kebiasaan dalam perang bahwa Jenderal selalu bertanggung jawab atas keberhasilan atau kekalahan pasukan, baik ia memainkan peran pribadi yang aktif dalam pertempuran maupun tidak. Meskipun sakit parah, Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas duduk di balkon dan terus memberikan perintah yang sangat membantu para Mujahidin. Ia terus berseru:

"Wahai Mujahidin, majulah ke kiri! Sekarang majulah ke kanan! Mughirah, kamu maju sekarang. Jarir, mundurlah sedikit sekarang.

Nu'man, seranglah dengan pedangmu. Asy'ats, majulah dan serang sekarang! Dan wahai Qa'qa', majulah dengan cepat sekarang!

Wahai pecinta Islam dan pengikut setia Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bagus sekali! Pastikan kalian tidak menyerah dan berhenti! Mata dunia tertuju pada kalian, mengagumi dan menyaksikan keberanian, keberanian, semangat, dan keinginan kalian untuk meraih syahadah!

Wahai Mujahidin! Kemenangan tinggal beberapa langkah lagi dan sedang menunggu kalian."

Kaum Muslim meraih kemenangan gemilang dalam Perang Qadisiyah, tetapi langkah kemenangan mereka masih jauh dari selesai.

Selanjutnya tentara Muslim menghadapi orang-orang Iran di Madyan. Dalam pertempuran ini pun orang-orang Iran datang dengan perlengkapan yang sangat baik dan persenjataan yang lengkap. Satu masalah besar bagi pasukan Muslim di sini adalah sungai Dajlah terletak di antara mereka dan tentara Iran. Orang-orang Iran telah menempatkan pasukan mereka di sepanjang tepi sungai dan mengambil posisi bertahan. Lebih jauh lagi, untuk melindungi diri mereka, mereka telah menghancurkan semua

jembatan di sungai tersebut. Untuk melakukan pertempuran yang menentukan, tentara Muslim harus bergerak lebih dulu dan maju. Jenderal Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas radhiyallahu 'anhu berada dalam dilema. Bagaimana ia harus menyeberangi sungai? Bahkan jika mereka berhasil menyeberangi sungai, sangat mungkin musuh akan melancarkan serangan bahkan sebelum mereka mencapai tepi sungai yang lain. Dalam hal itu musuh akan berada dalam posisi yang menguntungkan, karena tentara Muslim masih berada di sungai. Setelah banyak berpikir dan merencanakan, ia memutuskan untuk membentuk dua pasukan. Ia memerintahkan satu segmen untuk menyeberangi sungai di bawah komando 'Ashim bin 'Amr radhiyallahu 'anhu. Ia memerintahkan segmen lain dari pasukan untuk menyeberangi sungai dari jarak yang agak jauh; bagian kedua ini ia tempatkan di bawah Komando Qa'qa' bin 'Amr radhiyallahu 'anhu.

Tujuan strateginya adalah untuk membuat musuh sibuk dan terlibat dengan bagian pertama pasukan yang mencapai tepi sungai. Bagian lain dari pasukan akan mulai menyeberang setelah beberapa waktu berlalu, dan melancarkan serangan mematikan terhadap musuh segera setelah mencapai tepi sungai. Strategi ini begitu berhasil sehingga membuat orang-orang terkejut dan membangkitkan kekaguman mereka akan kecerdasannya. Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas radhiyallahu 'anhu telah menciptakan sejarah militer dengan langkah taktis yang baru ini.

Dengan keyakinan penuh kepada Allah Yang Maha Kuasa, ia telah meminta pasukannya untuk melangkah ke dalam sungai. Bibir mereka sibuk dengan lantunan Nama Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala. Mereka membaca ayat:

"Cukuplah Allah bagiku sebagai Penolong dan Pelindung."

Para Mujahidin telah melangkah ke dalam air yang dalam di sungai Dajlah tanpa rasa takut. Dunia menyaksikan bahwa mereka berbincang satu sama lain seolah-olah mereka sedang berjalan santai di daratan kering. Salman Al-Farisi juga seorang prajurit dalam pasukan ini. Ia mengungkapkan pemikiran yang indah dan menginspirasi pada kesempatan ini, yang layak ditulis dengan huruf emas dalam catatan sejarah. Ia berkata:

"Islam adalah agama yang sempurna dan turun dari langit. Aku bersumpah demi Allah Yang Maha Kuasa, bagi orang-orang beriman Islam, sungai ini sama sepele dengan gurun. Aku bersumpah demi Dzat yang di Tangan-Nya terletak hidupku bahwa Mujahidin yang keluar dengan selamat dari sungai sama banyaknya dengan mereka yang melangkah masuk ke sungai. Air sungai yang dalam tidak dapat membahayakan mereka dengan cara apa pun."

Sejarah menjadi saksi bahwa tidak ada seutas tali pun yang hilang dari para Mujahidin ketika mereka menyeberangi sungai. Sebuah cangkir jatuh dari tangan seorang Mujahid. Ia meminta rekan-rekannya untuk membantunya menemukannya; dan apa yang mereka lihat ternyata cangkir itu mengapung ke arah mereka di atas ombak. Segala puji bagi Allah! Allahu Akbar!

Melihat pemandangan yang menakjubkan dan menggetarkan ini, orang-orang Iran mulai berteriak bahwa raksasa-raksasa telah datang untuk menelan mereka dan melarikan diri dalam kepanikan. Namun Jenderal mereka tetap teguh. Beberapa orang yang tersebar berdiri di sisinya, tetapi orang-orang Iran menghadapi kekalahan total. Mereka dihancurkan pada hari pertama dan kota penting Madyan dengan istana-istana kerajaannya berhasil diduduki. Kekaisaran Iran telah melarikan diri ke Yazdagar. Semua barang dan harta rampasan dari Madyan dikirim ke Al-Madinah.

Ketika Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas radhiyallahu 'anhu memasuki kota Madyan di kepala pasukannya, ia menemukan kota itu benar-benar sepi dalam kesunyian yang mematikan. Ia begitu tersentuh sehingga membaca ayat-ayat berikut:

"Betapa banyak taman dan mata air yang mereka tinggalkan. Dan tanaman-tanaman hijau (ladang-ladang) dan tempat-tempat yang indah. Dan kesenangan hidup yang mereka nikmati. Demikianlah! Dan Kami mewariskannya kepada kaum yang lain (yaitu Kami menjadikan Bani Israil mewarisi kerajaan Mesir)." (QS. Ad-Dukhan: 25-28)

Segera setelah Madyan direbut, seluruh Irak berada di bawah kekuasaan kaum Muslim. Pemerintahan Islam mengumumkan amnesti umum, dan orang-orang yang telah meninggalkan rumah mereka karena takut diminta untuk kembali dan menetap di rumah mereka. Begitu orang-orang mendengar pengumuman ini, mereka kembali dan rumah, harta, serta kehormatan mereka dilindungi.

Setelah Irak berada di bawah kendali penuh pemerintah, Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas radhiyallahu 'anhu diangkat sebagai gubernur negara tersebut. Mengorganisir pemerintahan dan menjalankan negara sama sulitnya dengan menaklukkannya. Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas radhiyallahu 'anhu telah mengesankan orang-orang dengan keterampilan militer dan keberaniannya; kini sebagai Gubernur dan administrator, dengan perencanaan dan keterampilan organisasinya yang luar biasa, ia menjadikan Irak sebagai negara yang ideal dan patut dicontoh dalam waktu singkat.

Rasa keadilan dan sikap sportif, kemuliaan hati serta perlakuan baik terhadap semua orang membuat rakyat hidup bahagia dan puas. Namun sebagian penduduk Kufah yang keras kepala dan suka membuat masalah mengadukan kepada pemerintah pusat bahwa gubernur mereka tidak melaksanakan salah dengan cara yang benar dan menunjukkan kelalaian dalam ibadah.
content_ar: On the ثانياً day as well the المجاهدين triumphed over the العدو though it was nota decisive النصر. The ثالثاً day أيضاً it seemed as إذا the war was not going to end. The Iranian Empire continued to send in fresh troop everyday. The experienced general Qa‘q@’ bin ‘Amr adopted a new strategy on the ثالثاً day. He told a large number of his warriors to move towards Syria and stop there at a suitable spot. With the أولاً rays of dawn divisions consisting of a hundred الفرسان each should race their horses fast and reach the المعركةfield one by one. عندما these brigades, shouting ‘Allaého-Akbar’ reached, one بعد another, raising a lot of dust, the Iranian الجيش got terrified. They thought fresh reinforcements were coming in from Syria. But the Iranian Empire and his famous General, Rustam, were not prepared to admit الهزيمة. They stuck stubbornly in the المعركةfield. The الجيش had been positioned on the left and الحق of the elephants to protect them. But ‘Amr bin Ma’d Yakrab بعد informing his الصحابة, dared to attack the elephants. He did get injured in the process, but succeeded in cutting off the trunk of an elephant. All the other elephants stampeded, fleeing backwards. And ونتيجة لذلك, they trampled down their own soldiers. In spite of this it did not seem that the مسلم الجيش could الهزيمة the Iranians, who still had a very large number of soldiers. Qa’‘qa’ bin ‘Amr 4 formed a special division of ace riders and leading them himself, he advanced towards the Iranian General, Rustam. He was seated on his throne directing his men to fight. With one intense attack, he succeeded in reaching the throne of the Iranian General. He jumped down from his throne and tried to fight against them for somé time, but عندما he saw it was futile, he fled. عندما he saw a stream in front of him, he jumped into it to save himself; one of the المجاهدين, Hilal, who was chasing him jumped in بعد him and got stranglehold on him. He pulled him out of the stream by his legs, and chopped off his head with his السيف. It was then announced that the Iranian General had been killed. عندما the Iranian soldiers heard this they lost الأمل, and started to scamper from the field of المعركة. The المجاهدين chased them for a long distance and the المعركةfield was left empty. وهكذا, the مسلم الجيش won. Though Sa’d bin Abi Wagas . did not take an active part in the المعركة لأن of his المرض, he was continuously issuing orders, and for all practical purposes, commanding the forces. He was sitting on one of the balconies of the fort and could view the whole المعركةfield, the positions of the various divisions and the movements of the two armies. In this war, بالإضافة إلى the الإيمان and fervour of the المجاهدين what could be seen was the excellent planning of the القائد. ومن ثَم the credit for the النصر in the War of Qadisiyah went to the great General, Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas >. It has been the practice in wars that the General is always held responsible for the الفلاح or الهزيمة of the الجيش, whether he plays an active personal role in the المعركة or not. In spite of his serious المرض, Sa‘d bin Abi Waqds sat on the balcony and kept giving orders which helped the المجاهدين immensely. He kept calling out: “المجاهدين, advance towards the left! Now advance towards the الحق! Mughaira you move forward now. Jareer you retreat a little now. No’man attack with your السيف. Ash‘ath move forward and attack now! And O Qa’qa’, move forward fast now! O lovers of الإسلام and loyal followers of النبي ﷺ 3%, well done! See that you do not give up and stop! The الدنيا’s eyes are on you, envying you and watching your exploits of bravery, daring, fervour and desire for الشهيدdom! O المجاهدين! النصر is just a couple of steps away and waiting for you.” The المسلمون got a resounding النصر in the War of Qadisiyah, but their victorious march was far from over. Next the مسلم الجيش faced the Iranians in Madyan. In this المعركة أيضاً the Iranians came very well equipped and armed to the teeth. One major problem for the مسلم forces here was that the river Dajlah lay between them and the Iranian الجيش. The Iranians had deployed their الجيش along the banks of the river and adopted a defensive position. Further, من أجل protect themselves, they had destroyed all the bridges on the river. من أجل have a decisive encounter it was necessary that the مسلم الجيش make the أولاً move and advance. General Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas 4 was in a dilemma. How should he cross the river? Even إذا they did succeed in crossing the river, it was very possible the العدو would launch an attack even قبل they reached the other bank of the river. In that case the العدو would be at an advantage, لأن the مسلم الجيش would still be in the river. بعد a lot of thought and planning he decided to form two armies. He ordered one segment to cross the river under the command of ‘Aasim bin ‘Amr 4. He ordered the other segment of the الجيش to cross the river some distance away; this ثانياً الفصل he placed under the Command of Qa’qa’ bin ‘Amr #. The purpose of his strategy was to keep the العدو busy and involved with the أولاً الفصل of the الجيش reaching the bank. The other الفصل of the الجيش would start crossing بعد some time had elapsed, and make a deadly attack on the العدو as soon as it reached the shores. This strategy was لذا successful that it stunned people and aroused their admiration with its ingenuity. Sa’d bin Abi Waqas 4 had created military history with this novel tactical move. With full الإيمان in الله Almighty he had asked his men to step into the river. Their lips were busy with the incantation of the Name of الله 3B. They were reciting the Ayah: “الله’s Help and Support is enough for me.” The Mujaliideen had stepped into the deep waters of the river Dajlah fearlessly. The الدنيا saw that they were chatting with بعضهم بعضاً as إذا they were casually strolling on dry land. Salaman Al-Farsi was أيضاً a soldier in this الجيش. He expressed beautiful inspiring thoughts on this occasion, which deserve to be written in letters of gold in the annals of history. قال: “الإسلام is a religion which is perfect and descended from the heavens. | swear by the Almighty الله, for the المؤمنون of الإسلام, the river is as negligible as the desert. I swear by the Being in Whose Power lies my الحياة that as many المجاهدين will step out safely from the river as those who stepped into the river. The deep waters of the river can harm them in no الطريق.” History is witness to the fact that not even a rope was lost by the المجاهدين عندما they crossed the river. A cup fell from the hand of one Mujahid. He asked his الصحابة to help him find it; and what should they see but that the cup came floating towards them ona wave. All Praise is for الله! Alftah is Great! Seeing this fearful awe-inspiring sight, the Iranians started screaming, that giants had come to swallow them up and fled in panic. Their General, غير أن, remained resolute. A few scattered men stood by him, but the Iranians faced a total rout. They were decimated the very أولاً day and the المهم city of Madyan with its royal palaces was occupied. The Iranian Empire had already fled to Yazdagar. All the goods and treasures taken from Madyan were sent to A]-المدينة المنورة. عندما Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas + entered the city of Madyan at the head of his الجيش, he found the city totally deserted in deadly silence. He was لذا deeply moved that he recited the following الآيات: “How many of gardens and springs that they left behind. And green crops (fields) and goodly places. And comforts of الحياة wherein they used to take delight. وهكذا (it was)! And We made other people inherit them (i.e. We made the الأولاد of Israel to inherit the kingdom of Egypt).” (44:25-28) As soon as Madyan was captured the whole of Iraq came under the sway of the المسلمون. The Islamic government declared a general amnesty, and people who had fled their houses out of الخوف were asked to return and settle in their homes. As soon as people heard this announcement they came back and their homes, their الثروة and their honour were protected. بعد Iraq came under the complete control of the government, Sa’d bin Abi Waqas 4% was appointed governor of the country. It is as difficult to organise a government and run the country as it is to conquer it. Sa’d bin Abi Waqas % had impressed people with his military skill and الشجاعة; now as Governor and administrator, with his excellent planning and organisational skills, he made Iraq an ideal and exemplary state in a short time. The sense of العدل and fair play, the magnanimity and الخير treatment of all people made the people happy and contented. But some of the Koofis who were stubborn trouble mongers complained to the government at the centre that their governor did not السلوك الصلوات in a proper manner and displays laxity in devotions.
